records.correctedComment: Static retail identity: CBattleEngineWalkerPart::UpdateWalkCycle. Called only from CBattleEngineWalkerPart__Move, this ECX-receiver copies walk cycle +0x24 to old cycle +0x28, reads the main part at +0x20, transposes its orientation basis, obtains velocity through vtable slot +0x6c, projects velocity into local space, advances the cycle using the larger-magnitude X/Y component, and wraps through plus or minus pi. records.rationale: The 76-instruction retail body is a direct match for CBattleEngineWalkerPart::UpdateWalkCycle. Its only caller is CBattleEngineWalkerPart__Move; the receiver layout is WalkerPart-shaped (+0x20 main part, +0x24 walk cycle, +0x28 old cycle). The body copies +0x24 to +0x28, transposes the linked main-part orientation, obtains velocity through vtable slot +0x6c, projects it into local space, advances the cycle using the larger-magnitude X/Y component, and wraps through plus or minus pi exactly as BattleEngineWalkerPart.cpp. The saved comment incorrectly says it chooses the smaller component. Plain RET and ECX-only use prove no explicit stack arguments; zero-argument __fastcall and __thiscall are machine-equivalent here, so this review preserves the observed fastcall spelling instead of claiming an unobservable convention change. This is static retail identity plus architecture/name alignment only; concrete layouts, runtime gait behavior, BEA patch behavior, and rebuild parity remain separate proof.

records.correctedComment: Wave398 signature/comment correction: pair dispatcher warns and returns when this+0x04 is non-null and the dword at [this+0x04]+0x0c exceeds 0x3b6. It samples both owners' centre positions with CThing__GetCentrePos and computes centre distance minus the two component radii. For positive separation it estimates an arrival time from both owners' vtable+0x3c speed-like values and DAT_00672fd0; a zero combined speed returns. If the estimate is later than DAT_00672fd0, it schedules EVENT_MANAGER event 2000 for that time. Otherwise it calls CHLCollisionDetector__HandleCollisionEnter immediately when this+0x10 is zero, or schedules event 2000 with time -1.0f when this+0x10 is nonzero. Either scheduled path reuses the saved event pointer at this+0x0c when present and then clears that pointer. Static retail evidence only; exact list/field meanings, speed callback identity, time units, runtime collision behavior, and rebuild parity remain unproven.
records.rationale: The recovered variants agree on the scheduling role but inherit the routed final's claim that the function compares targeting positions. The raw calls CThing__GetCentrePos for both owners, computes centre distance minus their component radii, derives an arrival estimate from two vtable +0x3c speed-like values and DAT_00672fd0, and then selects an immediate-enter or event-2000 path. The comment therefore needs an exact centre-position correction and tighter control-flow wording.

records.correctedComment: Wave398 follow-up: map/who sweep compares previous versus current sector coordinates across descending layers; when they differ, scans the 3x3 neighborhood around the current sector while excluding cells still within Chebyshev distance 1 of the prior sector. On the current top layer it traverses quad children and calls CHLCollisionDetector__DispatchFilteredCollisionPair for MapWho candidates; on lower layers it applies mutual +0x20 filters and calls CHLCollisionDetector__DispatchCollisionEventForPair, warning instead when this+0x10 is 1. records.correctedComment: Wave421 signature/comment hardening: when global mip level 0x006fabf0 is nonzero, shifts the caller tile_rect by that mip, selects shared texture 0x006fabf4 or the per-instance texture at +0x08 according to 0x006fabcc, and attempts a vtable +0x4c lock; only a nonnegative lock result copies RGB565 pixels from landscape buffer 0x0067a7d8 with mip-scaled strides. The tail invokes selected texture vtable +0x50 unconditionally, including global-mip-zero and lock-failure paths. Raw control flow makes the distinction material: the global-mip-zero branch at 0x0048e95f and the lock-failure branch at 0x0048e9bb both jump to the shared tail at 0x0048ea42, which selects the shared or instance texture and invokes vtable slot +0x50. The current comment scopes unlock under the active-mip lock/copy sentence, so it misses unconditional unlock on both bypass paths.

records.correctedComment: Wave471 signature/comment hardening: CPlayer::GetOldCurrentViewPoint source bridge. The retail body indexes the camera table through this+0x2c. When a camera exists, it calls camera slot +0x08 and copies four returned dwords to out_old_view_point. When no camera exists, it writes zero to output dwords +0x00/+0x04/+0x08 but copies an uninitialized fourth local dword to output +0x0c, so the fallback is not a fully zeroed 16-byte value. RET 0x4 confirms one output-pointer stack argument. Static retail/source evidence only; exact FVector layout, camera-table indexing, runtime camera behavior, and rebuild parity remain unproven.
records.rationale: Both recovered variants independently flag the same overstatement in the final comment. Raw instructions initialize only stack dwords later copied to output +0x00, +0x04, and +0x08; on the null-camera branch they load output +0x0c from untouched [ESP+0x18]. The no-camera fallback therefore zeros XYZ but does not produce a fully zeroed 16-byte value, while the camera-present branch still calls slot +0x08 and copies four returned dwords.

Platform__LoadAndUploadMappedTextureWrapper loads its third stack argument into EAX before the call; this helper immediately copies EAX into ESI, so ESI is not an independent implicit caller input. The address-suffixed name remains appropriately bounded, but the current comment's undifferentiated implicit EAX/ESI wording is inaccurate, and the saved stack-only prototype cannot represent the EAX-carried input.

records.correctedComment: Wave681 static read-back: cdecl varargs diagnostic helper always sets the directive-parser context error flag at +0x2c. When +0x38 is nonzero, it compares diagnostic_format with the literal string syntax error; that branch emits either CTexture__LogUnexpectedTokenError or an invalid-preprocessor-command diagnostic based on current token state. The routed final instead says it special-cases syntax-error tokens. records.correctedComment: Wave721 static read-back: packed matrix4x4 inverse variant called by the scalar optional-transform composition path. It computes determinant/cofactor terms and writes the determinant when the optional output pointer is non-null. A packed equality comparison detects a zero determinant; on that path it skips inverse writes and returns the low comparison mask 0xffffffff (-1). Otherwise it writes the reciprocal-determinant-scaled inverse matrix and returns the output pointer as an int-compatible value. Static retail Ghidra metadata/decompile/instruction/xref evidence only; exact dispatch-table slot schema, packed vector/matrix storage contract, source identity, runtime math correctness, BEA patching, and rebuild parity remain unproven.
records.currentSignature: int __stdcall CFastVB__DispatchOp_InvertMatrix4x4_Variant_005a9637(float * out_inverse_matrix4x4, float * out_determinant_or_null, float * input_matrix4x4)
records.rationale: Both recovered variants repeat the final comment's claim that a zero determinant returns zero, but the raw epilogue disproves it. PFCMPEQ at 0x005a98b3 compares a packed zero register with the determinant, MOVD at 0x005a98bf transfers the low equality mask to EAX, and JNZ at 0x005a98d5 skips inverse stores; the common epilogue then returns that unchanged 0xffffffff mask. The nonsingular path instead loads the output pointer into EAX, writes the inverse, and returns that pointer-compatible value.

records.correctedComment: Wave739 static read-back, refreshed by final semantic review: HRESULT-to-C-string mapper used by D3D/render/texture error paths. The current final xref export contains 21 unconditional call sites; the adjacent Direct3DCreate9 row contributes the 22nd xref in the old two-target aggregate. The body reads hresult from [ESP+0x4], selects among 3,497 unique literal-string returns through comparisons and jump tables, includes E_ABORT, falls back to Unknown, and ends at its single RET 0x4 at 0x005c9c66. The original 60-second Ghidra decompile timed out; a later read-only 300-second decompile of a disposable trusted-final copy succeeded and confirmed the mapper, after which the copy was removed with no source-project difference. Static retail evidence only; exact HRESULT table completeness, runtime error-reporting behavior, BEA patching, and rebuild parity remain unproven.
records.rationale: The deleted exact verdict remains unrecovered; only the shard summary is available. Independent final evidence nevertheless resolves the semantic question: the 9,817-instruction body reads hresult from [ESP+0x4], contains no calls, selects 3,497 immediate EAX string pointers through comparison/jump-table logic, and ends at its only RET 0x4 at 0x005c9c66. The original 60-second decompile timed out, while a fresh read-only 300-second decompile on a disposable trusted-final copy succeeded, produced 3,497 unique literal-string returns including E_ABORT plus the Unknown fallback, left the copied project exactly matching its source, and was then removed. The current xref export has 21 calls to HResultToString and one call to adjacent Direct3DCreate9; therefore the saved comment's 22-call-site claim mistakenly promotes the old two-target aggregate. The name and stdcall char-pointer signature remain supported, but the count requires a comment-only correction; runtime behavior remains unproven.
